channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Desperation amid search for survivors of an airstrike on a crowded area near major Beirut hospital
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Nearly 16 hours after an Israeli airstrike hit across the street from Beirut’s main public hospital, rescuers were still removing debris Tuesday from the overcrowded slum area.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: It could take 350 years for Gaza to rebuild if it remains under a blockade, UN report says
item.description: United Nations agencies have long warned that it could take decades to rebuild Gaza after Israel's offensive against Hamas, one of the deadliest and most destructive military campaigns since World War II. Now, more than a year into the war, a new report speaks in terms of centuries. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea warns it can send arms to Ukraine after reports of North's troops in Russia
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea warned Tuesday it could consider supplying weapons to Ukraine in response to North Korea allegedly dispatching troops to Russia, as both North Korea and Russia denied the movements. NATO's secretary general said that would mark a “significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran Revolutionary Guard officer charged in plot to kill American author in US
item.description: The Justice Department unsealed an indictment against four Iranian officials Tuesday for their alleged involvement in a murder-for-hire targeting Iranian-American journalist Masih Alinejad.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: France defends restrictions on Israeli firms supplying Middle East wars and says it's not a boycott
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France's government on Tuesday defended its decision to bar Israeli companies supplying the wars in the Middle East from exhibiting at an upcoming trade fair outside Paris. Organizers of the Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lee Kuan Yew's youngest son says the UK has granted him asylum from persecution in Singapore
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Lee Hsien Yang, the estranged brother of former Singapore Prime Minister Lee Hsien Loong, said Tuesday that he is now a “political refugee” after the U.K. government granted him asylum from what he described as persecution at home.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and EILEEN NG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A gasoline truck overturns and explodes near Uganda's capital, killing 11
item.description: Police in Uganda say least 11 people including children were killed when a fuel truck exploded next to a highway on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Muhumuza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 transgender women stabbed to death at home in northwest Pakistan
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two men armed with daggers killed two transgender women at their home in conservative northwest Pakistan overnight before fleeing the scene, police said, a sign of increasing violence against trans people in the country.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy says a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway Germany
item.description: Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his “victory plan” after the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: About 140 Rohingya Muslims on wooden boat off Indonesia's coast as residents refuse to let them land
item.description: Indonesian officials say about 140 weak and hungry Rohingya Muslims, mostly women and children, are on a wooden boat anchored about 1 mile off the coast of Indonesia’s northernmost province of Aceh, and local residents refused to allow them onto land.
item.dc:creator: Binsar Bakkara And Edna Tarigan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Binational delegation heads to Mexico City to talk sewage, border waits, stalled port of entry
item.description: The delegation is led by the San Diego Regional Chamber of Commerce, which says their mission is to "champion U.S.-Mexico relations and advance issues critical to the success of the binational business community as the newly sworn-in Mexico Administration begins its session.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Peru’s ex-president Toledo gets more than 20 years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel says it killed a Hezbollah official expected to become the group's next leader
item.description: The Israeli military says one of its airstrikes in early October killed a top Hezbollah official who had been widely expected to become the group’s next leader.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In annual religious pilgrimage, Panamanians crawl to reach the Black Christ of Portobelo
item.description: PORTOBELO, Panama (AP) — At dawn, pilgrims like Ernesto Troya undertook the hardest stretch of their journey to reach the statue of the Black Christ of Portobelo in Panama. Moved by faith, they crawled for several hours along the street toward the image of the saint.
item.dc:creator: MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gunmen kill 7 people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Gunmen fatally shot at least seven people working on a strategic tunnel project in Indian-controlled Kashmir and injured at least five others, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense chief promises Ukraine what it needs to fight Russia but goes no further
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The United States “will get Ukraine what it needs” to fight its war with Russia, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds mourn Catholic priest and Indigenous peace activist killed in southern Mexico
item.description: SAN ANDRES LARRAINZAR, Mexico (AP) — Hundreds gathered Monday to mourn Catholic priest Marcelo Pérez, an activist for Indigenous peoples and farm laborers who was killed in the southern Mexico state of Chiapas.
item.dc:creator: RAÚL VERA and ISABEL MATEOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken heads to Middle East in wake of Sinwar's killing
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is going to the Middle East in the wake of the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Voting from abroad: What to know
item.description: Most U.S. citizens who reside outside the country can vote absentee in primary and general elections.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Austin warns 'no silver bullet' to defeat Putin as US aid hangs in balance ahead of elections
item.description: Secretary of Defense Austin said there is "no silver bullet" to defeat Russian President Putin and urged the global community to bolster aid to Ukraine as concern mounts over continued U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American reportedly kidnapped in Philippine coastal town; police investigating
item.description: Police in the Philippines are searching for American national Elliot Onil Eastman, from Vermont, after receiving reports gunmen had kidnapped him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF releases video showing Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar fleeing into tunnel with family before Oct. 7 attack
item.description: The IDF released footage showing Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar fleeing underground into a tunnel in Gaza in the hours leading up to the Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel begins strikes against Lebanon targeting Hezbollah financial assets
item.description: The Israeli Defense Forces began conducting airstrikes in Lebanon on Sunday targeting financial institutions linked to the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sanders: Trump will be 'closer to Netanyahu' on Gaza if reelected
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said Sunday he believes former President Trump will have similar views of the Israel-Hamas war to those of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he is reelected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Greek man convicted for sneaking onto neighbors' properties to smell their shoes
item.description: A Greek judge gave a man a suspended one-month prison sentence following his conviction for sneaking onto his neighbors' properties to smell their shoes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba hit by second blackout after grid collapse
item.description: Cubans experienced their second black out wave Saturday after government officials said they were working to restore electrical service to hospitals and crucial service centers after a grid failure on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 27 in Lebanon, including in a town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: QANA, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes pounded areas across Lebanon, killing at least 27 people over the past 24 hours, officials said Wednesday, including more than a dozen in a southern town where Israeli bombardments in previous conflicts are seared into local memory.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD ZAATARI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation
